chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.16 of 2026 Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 05.03.2026 Mr. Muhammad Zahir, advocate for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Salim, Addl: PG Sindh O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.210/2025, u/s 9(i) 3 (b) CNS Amend. Act, 2022, registered at Police Station Shahrah-e-Noor Jahan, Karachi by means of this application. 2. Applicant was arrested on 26.03.2025 by a police team headed by ASI Muhammad Khan Kanjha of Police Station Shahrah-e-Noor Jahan, from main road nusrat Bhutto Mor opposite Rangers Chowki, North Nazimabad, Karachi, and from him allegedly 540 grams of charas was recovered. Hence, he was booked in the present case. 3. Learned counsel for the applicant has pleaded for bail on the ground that the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr. PC; the challan has been submitted and the applicant is no more required for further investigation. 4. Learned Addl: PG has opposed the bail application on the ground that the applicant has history of previous criminal cases. However, learned defence counsel has submitted that in those previous cases the applicant has already been granted bail. In view of the above facts as well as the fact that the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr. PC, and applicant is no more required for further investigation, and there is prima facie no record of compliance u/s 17 Sindh Control of Narcotics Substances Act, 2024.

In view of the above facts as well as the fact that the alleged offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause u/s 497 Cr. PC, and applicant is no more required for further investigation, and there is prima facie no record of compliance u/s 17 Sindh Control of Narcotics Substances Act, 2024. This application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/ (Rupees one hundred thousand only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
